---
toc: false
format:
  html:
    css: index_style.css
    code-copy: false
---

```{=html}
      <div class="main-container">
            <h2>A Hub for Collaborative <br> Data Science Education</h2>
            <p>Explore open-source projects and modular curriculum <br> 
                developed by the Kansas Data Science Consortium <br> 
                Designed to be adapted for any classroom.</p>
            
            <button class = "explore-button"><a href="./project.qmd" style="text-decoration:none; color:white">Explore Projects</a></button>
            
            <button class = "explore-button"><a href ="./curriculum.qmd" style="text-decoration:none; color:#0051ba">Browse Curriculum</a></button>
        </div>

        <div class="cards-wrapper">
            <div class ="project-container">
                <h2>Projects</h2>
                <p>The Community Data Lab is our signature program which brings real-world projects into the classroom. This section houses deidentified data, analysis code, materials, and project reports from previous semesters.</p>
                <a href = "./project.qmd">View projects &#8594;</a>
            </div>

            <div class ="curriculum-container">
                <h2>Curriculum</h2>
                <p>Our curriculum repository contains content from introductory classes to advanced machine learning. This includes lecture materials, analysis code, exercises, and datasets.</p>
                <a href = "./curriculum.qmd">Browse Modules &#8594;</a>
            </div>
        </div> <!-- End of wrapper div -->


        <div class="how-works-container">
                <h2 >How to Use This Repository</h2>
                <p >A simple, three-step process for educators.</p>
                <div class="steps-grid">
                    <!-- Step 1 -->
                    <div class="steps-grid-step1">
                        <i class="bi bi-search"></i>
                        <h4>1. Discover</h4>
                        <p>Find real-world projects from our Community Data Lab or browse our modular curriculum.</p>
                    </div>
                    <!-- Step 2 -->
                    <div class="steps-grid-step2">
                        <i class="bi bi-box-arrow-down"></i>
                        <h4>2. Adapt</h4>
                        <p>All materials are open-source. Adapt them for your own courses and institutional needs.</p>
                    </div>
                    <!-- Step 3 -->
                    <div class="steps-grid-step3">
                        <i class="bi bi-book-half"></i>
                        <h4>3. Implement</h4>
                        <p>Integrate our projects and modules to enrich your data science curriculum.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
        </div>
```
